localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarDescription: Learn how to manage story tags and status cards.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarIntroContent: The Story Tag Sidebar replaces the combat tracker with a shared space for managing story tags and status cards. It's the central place to track narrative elements during play.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarTabContent:
The story tag sidebar lives here, in the tags tab of the sidebar. Click this icon to open it at any time.

Tip: Right-click this icon to pop the sidebar out into its own window — handy for keeping tags visible alongside other panels.

Keyboard shortcut: T (pops out / closes the sidebar window)
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarStorySectionContent: The top section holds story tags — shared narrative tags visible to everyone. These represent things in the fiction that any character might interact with.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarAddButtonsContent: Use to add a story tag (a simple narrative fact) or to add a status card (a tracked condition with tiers).
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarTagItemContent: A story tag is a narrative fact — e.g. "Dark Alley" or "Raining". Click the name to edit it. The hourglass marks a tag as single-use, meaning it burns after one use.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarStatusItemContent: A status card tracks a condition with up to 6 tiers. Check boxes to raise the tier. Statuses work like tags in rolls but carry escalating weight.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarAddActorContent: Drag actors from the sidebar or click Add Actor to track per-character tags and statuses. Each actor gets their own section with the same tag and status tools.
localization.TOURS.StoryTagSidebarSummaryContent:
Use story tags and statuses to track the evolving fiction. Players can drag tags from here onto their roll dialog to include them.

Right-click any tag for options like burn, convert, or remove.

T = pop out / close the sidebar window
